Directed the organisation's efforts to combat gender-based violence in the Afar region of Ethiopia through programmes that promote girls' enrolment in education and post-secondary pathways, as well as initiatives advancing women's financial inclusion by establishing a women's cooperatives.

Led a team of 12 people to establish the company from the ground up.

_extracted.professional_summary: Yesu Girma Taye is a seasoned finance professional with a distinguished career spanning the banking, digital financial services, microfinance, and agribusiness sectors. Known for his sharp analytical skills and strategic problem-solving abilities, Yesu has a proven track record of delivering tailored financial solutions that drive organizational growth and long-term sustainability. His expertise lies in blending technical finance acumen with business leadership to tackle complex challenges, enabling organizations to thrive in dynamic and often volatile markets. Over the course of his career, Yesu has successfully led high-impact investments and conducted in-depth feasibility studies across multiple industries, including agribusiness, construction, and financial services. His leadership has been instrumental in guiding organizations toward financial stability, operational efficiency, and scalable growth, particularly through innovative financing solutions. His strategic oversight and financial insight have not only unlocked new revenue streams but also enhanced organizational resilience in the face of economic challenges. One of Yesu's core strengths is his ability to manage projects that demand a deep understanding of regulatory environments, complex financial modeling, and stakeholder coordination. His approach to problem-solving is holistic—he considers financial, operational, and strategic factors to craft solutions that are both pragmatic and forward-looking. Whether establishing new microfinance institutions, securing capital for large-scale investments, or preparing businesses for investment readiness, Yesu consistently delivers measurable results. His ability to work seamlessly with international teams across diverse cultural contexts has further solidified his reputation as a global leader in financial consulting. Yesu's respect for diversity, combined with his adaptability, ensures that he excels in multicultural environments, making him a highly effective advisor in both local and international projects.

employment.description_of_duties: As the Project Team Leader for the Ethiopia Connect Project at AMAS, I play a pivotal role in driving strategic initiatives to enhance financial inclusion, particularly access to finance for smallholder farmers in Ethiopia. I am leading the establishment of a digital-led microfinance institution (MFI) in collaboration with both national and international consortium parties of my project by maintaining respect for cultural and individual diversities. My leadership was instrumental in raising 200% of the required capital to launch the MFI. Additionally, I manage stakeholder relationships, represent the organization in key meetings and oversee multiple high-value financial models and feasibility studies.

At Resilience BV, I spearheaded an assignment within the framework of the Ethio-Netherlands Seed Partnership project. My primary responsibility was to develop an in-depth financing strategy tailored specifically for seed-producing companies. This role entailed a thorough assessment of the financial needs and challenges faced by these companies, and devising a strategy that would enable them to access essential financial services. My approach involved identifying funding sources that would support the growth and scaling of seedling operations. By ensuring that these companies had the financial backing needed, I contributed in fostering their development and success within the agricultural sector.

As a Freelance Consultant at Precise Consult, I successfully led a significant assignment commissioned by the International Finance Corporation, which was centered around the development of an oilseed sourcing and financing strategy. I analyzed market trends, financial flows, and sourcing mechanisms to craft a comprehensive strategy that not only met the project's objectives but also provided actionable insights and recommendations. My contributions were instrumental in shaping a strategy that facilitated efficient sourcing and financing processes, thereby supporting the broader goals of the IFC's initiative. As a result, IFC can design a financial product that benefits more than 150,000 smallholder farmers.

_extracted.professional_summary: Associate Professor in the Department of Economics at Addis Ababa University with a Ph.D. in Economics from Göteborg University, Sweden. Senior Research Fellow at AEMFI/EIFTRI with extensive expertise in resource and environmental economics, finance, monetary economics, microfinance, poverty analysis, public economics, and governance. Dr. Alemu has over 40 years of professional experience, including teaching both graduate and undergraduate level courses in advanced microeconomics, natural resource economics, econometrics, and quantitative methods. He has led numerous research surveys and published extensively in peer-reviewed journals and conferences on topics including land tenure, climate change adaptation, financial inclusion, forest management decentralization, and agricultural household decision-making. His research interests span property rights, land tenure security, microfinance impacts on resilience, and climate-smart agricultural practices in Ethiopia and the broader East African region.

employment.employer: Bilateral Program between Netherlands Government, Embassy of Kingdom of Netherlands (EKN) and Ethiopian Government, Ministry of Finance
employment.description_of_duties:
BENEFIT used to test and validate technologies, practices, and approaches as well as build the capacity of relevant stakeholders in agriculture and trade in partnership with local universities, agriculture research institutes, agriculture offices and public and private enterprises. The program is implemented in Ethiopia with support of WAGENINGEN University and Research (WUR) in four regions (Amhara, Oromia, Tigray and SNNPR). There were five programs under BENEFIT umbrella and my responsibility in BENEFIT is to ensure gender and Nutrition Sensitive Agriculture is embedded during planning, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of each program, such as Integrated Seed Sector Development (ISSD), Capacity Building and Scaling Up of Evidence Based Approach (CASCAPE), Sesame Business Network Development project (SBN), Ethiopian Netherlands Trade for Agricultural Growth (ENTAG) and Realizing Sustainable Agricultural Livelihood Security (REALISE).
A. Gender and Nutrition Mainstreaming and Specific strategies
Responsible for developing gender and nutrition mainstreaming policy, strategies, standards, norms and guidance/technical for the BENEFIT Partnership.
Ensure gender and nutrition is embedded in the different programs of the BENEFIT Partnership. I was responsible to support and stimulate the integration of gender and nutrition related issues into the project plans and monitor the progress of activities. And supports gender analysis and tailor-made support for each project.
Support the nutrition-agriculture linkages and contribute to the development of the annual work plan and technical decisions as well as for program management, implementation, and monitoring, in compliance with the defined individual BENEFIT programs strategies and approaches.
B. Sensitization and Communication
Promote nutrition and gender sensitive learning and innovations among and outside the BENEFIT Partnership units. This includes promoting networking and partnership with government and partners to promote gender and nutrition in their programs and interventions via different platforms.
Provide gender and nutrition trainings and coaching to BENEFIT staff and partners involved. I'm responsible for regular visit to regional staff to engage on gender and nutrition thematic areas.
In collaboration with the M & E and Communication, develop appropriate gender and nutrition measurement indicators and track progress and success stories based on the established indicators.
Develop tailored messages sharing the results of BENEFIT on gender and nutrition with various audiences.
Provide regular advice to the BENEFIT Management team on gender and nutrition issues and get their collaboration to strengthen the two themes in their intervention.
C. Monitoring and Evaluation and Reporting
Follow up of the implementation of the proposed activities and documentation of the best practices in mainstreaming gender in programs. This includes conducting regular gender and nutrition impact audit together with the M & E expert and in consultation with the BENEFIT management Team.
Together with BENEFIT manager and projects' staff on gender and nutrition, feed gender and nutrition results into a collaboration monitoring framework.
Responsible for internal and external evaluation of all projects and programs under the BENEFIT Partnership.
Accountable for tracking progresses and publicizing the results achieved and communicating to financer and other relevant stakeholders. I should also consolidate the monitoring and analysis of the collaboration.
Produce quarterly progress reports and annual reports on gender sensitive nutrition.
Achievements
Scalable experiences of BENEFIT programs on gender and nutrition captured, published and communicated to Policy Makers, for example use of PICS bags proven successful in KOCHO fermentation, ENSET scraper: small investment with multiple benefits, Gender mainstreaming contribution to women's empowerment in informal seed system development, Food Safety and Quality issues in poultry sector of Ethiopia, case of BENEFIT ENTAG-(all publications are found in www.benefitethiopia.org and www.academia.edu )

A. Capacity Building and Technical Support
Took active part in project design, planning, implementation, and monitoring to ensure that adequate attention is paid (and resources allocated) to the promotion of gender equality across emergency projects.
Developed gender in emergencies training module and tools to build the capacity of staffs, government, and community and implementing partners.
B. Coordination and Representation
Ensured inter-departmental synergy between emergency unit and other units, programs, and projects.
Coordinated regularly with the rest of gender advisors and technical team to ensure that activities and strategies are implemented in a coherent and coordinated way with other program activities.
Built strategic alliances with other key actors internally and externally to advocate for gender-sensitive programming.
C. Promote Evidence Based Gender Programming
Periodically conducted gender analysis and assessment on impact of the project in economic and social empowerment of project beneficiaries including VSLA and self-help groups
Monitored project activities through the gender lens and contributed for improvement.
Ensured that issues of GBV and SEA are explored in sensitive ways, and that services and follow-up are provided in keeping with CARE's guidelines and policies.
D. Monitoring and evaluation
Developed approach and tools for documenting lessons learned on gender and women's empowerment issues.
Collected and promoted good practices and lessons learned, contributing to a collection of applicable good practices for gender equality programming.
Achievements
Conducted Rapid Gender Analysis (RGA)
The RGA was used during project design, implementation, and evaluation.
The RGA was communicated to relevant stakeholders to enhance the understanding of key partners about gender in emergencies (theory & practice) and improve coordination with local partners in the area of gender in emergencies.
Training on concept of gender, why gender matter in emergencies, and Gender Handbook in Humanitarian Action that is developed by Inter-agency Standing Committee (IASC) was communicated to CARE emergency staff and key stakeholders.

Managing the Empowering the source gender project.
Providing leadership in the design and implementation of gender thematic strategies, such as (1) improve health and wellbeing of flower farms workers (80% young women) by promoting peer education system, The health component includes personal hygiene, food and water hygiene, nutrition, sexual and reproductive health and family planning, (2) create gender sensitive management at farm level, (3) Support farm representatives in developing communication channels with local stakeholders to prevent and respond to sexual harassment and gender based violence.
Facilitating the Institutionalization of Gender Equality across EHPEA, and extending support to member farms
Leading a baseline assessment, (preparation of a concept note that includes data collection methodologies and time frame and tools, hired data enumerators (qualitative and quantitative) and encoders, trained data enumerators, logistic arrangements, writing up of the assessment report.
Executed training, such as making editorial during preparation of various training manuals and posters on health and gender-based violence topics in local languages, developing training work plan and follow up the delivery of the training as per the work plan.
Recruited, supervised, coached EHPEA gender and health team.
Coordinate and lead the development of annual plan and budget.
Provided trainings for farm management on gender and gender mainstreaming, facilitated workshops, and edutainment programs.
Supported farms to develop a gender committee and gender policy at farm level, harassment policy & procedure, (support includes development and provision of various tools and close follow up and supervision).
Followed up the benchmarking of social requirements against international standards and EHPEA Code of Practice.
Developed and maintained sustainable partnerships and alliances with Civil Society organizations and Social Movements to advance EHPEA's work on gender and health.
Capitalized success stories promoting gender work to internal and external audiences.
Achievements:
Establishment of a gender department as part of EHPEA organogram, and recruited staffs with relevant qualifications.
Engagement of farms to implement gender project in their farms.
Baseline assessment (qualitative and quantitative) conducted to inform the project plan, strategy and M&E framework
Farms show initiative to establish gender committee, formulation of gender policy and "zero tolerance" harassment policy.
Peer discussion system established at farm level on health and harassment issues, health topic includes personal hygiene, food and water hygiene, nutrition, sexual and reproductive health and family planning etc...
Linkages established between farms and relevant stakeholders to prevent and respond to GBV by establishing forums at farm's vicinity. The forums provided opportunities for farms to utilize some government resources such as medical services (health bureaus), social services and training (bureau of social and labour affairs, and bureau of women and children) and legal services (bureaus of Police and prosecutors).
Awareness about GBV created through edutainment programs conducted to farm workers with the presence of farm management, farm workers, representatives from government bodies.

I used to be a member of change management Directorate in FEACC. The Directorate was established following the business process re-engineering conducted in the commission in 2009. I was promoted to as a change agent, and my responsibilities were: 1. Communicate and promote the benefits of organizational change at all levels in line with the new vision of the organization. 2. Encourage participatory ways of working with relevant stakeholders such that there is a sustained level of engagement and buy-in of the new organizational vision statement. 3. Set clear and measurable objectives of organizational change actions with milestones and check their progress on a continuous basis through self-assessment; contribute to monitoring the implementation of the change process. 4. Develop work plans to facilitate the implementation of organizational change in the relevant change areas. 5. Facilitate the development of practical proposals for organizational change in line with the vision statement and strategic framework to address the organizational issues and advise / propose mechanisms for implementation to achieve full ownership. 6. Communicate clearly at all levels during each step of the organizational change process. 7. Assist in providing both advice and concrete suggestions to the strategic management body or board of directors responsible for supporting the organizational change initiative. 8. I used to be a member of the Commission's management committee and used to take minute of the management committee meeting
Actively engaged and ensured gender mainstreaming in strategic and annual plan preparation and implementation of the commission.
Facilitated and organized in-house and external gender related trainings to the Commission staff and management members so as to enhance gender sensitivity in the commissions.
Celebrated gender related events such as 16 days activism against GBV and March 8 to created awareness and advocated and lobbied the management to put in place gender sensitive organizational policies.
Promoted local and international experiences regarding integration of gender in anti-corruption activities for colleagues and top management members.
Undertook joint regular monitoring and evaluation to identify gender gaps.
Participated on the regular monitoring to assess the progress and proper implementation of gender and gender sensitive policies, programs and systems.
Organized mentoring and backstopping sessions to improve the gap identified during monitoring and evaluation.
Participated in research activities conducted in the area of gender and anti-corruption, such as gender audit.
Developed sex disaggregated data gathering system together with Data administrators.
Published plans, activities and achievements regarding gender in the Commission's newsletter.
Used to organize training to empower women staff in the Commission, such as women in leadership, women assertiveness and decision making, etc.

I was a member of Ethics Education and Public Relations Department from December 10, 2004, to April 22, 2007, at FEACC. During this time, I used to produce and publish news and articles on governance, ethics education and anti-corruption issues for "Ethics" magazine. I used to produce educational TV and Radio spots that were transferred on National TV and radio, produced articles for brochures, flyers, and the Commission's website. I used to produce opinion and commentaries and published on Ethiopian Herald newspaper. Later, I request to be transferred to Research Department of the Commission and my request was accepted. Since April 23, 2007, to December 3, 2012, I used to conduct applied qualitative and quantitative audience research as a result the research used to help to change the commission's media and communication strategy, inform media plans, and monitor achievements.

Being a member of Ethics Education and Public Relations Department, I used to produce and publish news and articles for the Commissions media and communication tools both in Amharic and English languages. The Commission media tools include magazine, brochure and fliers. In addition, I used to produce narrative spots on ethics and anti-corruption issues that were transferred on national radio and television.

As journalist, used to produce articles and news for weekly published newspaper

_extracted.professional_summary: Result oriented, ITIL and PMP certified Information Technology executive with more than 21 years of proven track record of delivering IT business solutions and operations across commercial and government institutions. Currently working as Data/IT Advisor within the Ethiopia Ministry of Revenues Tax Transformation Program, responsible for delivering a series of tax transformation projects designed to improve the ease of doing business (eodb) ranking of Ethiopia, specifically improvements in paying tax. Previously served as Chief IT Officer (CIO) and E-Trading Project Manager within Ethiopia Commodity Exchange where successfully managed the software requirement analysis, design, development and implementation of the entire commodity trading platform software application (Electronic Trading Software) from ground-up for Africa's fastest growing Commodity Exchange in Ethiopia. Possesses more than 8 years of experience as a software developer with expertise across multiple programming languages, databases, and enterprise platforms. Key achievements include preparing To-be process maps and business requirement specifications for 10 domestic tax processes, upgrading Electronic Tax Services and resolving critical software bugs, developing a comprehensive Information Technology strategic plan (2020-2025), and implementing robust software design and development across multiple enterprise applications.

Designed, developed and implemented ECX-clearing and settlement application software which was used by the exchange as a tool for clearing and settlement of daily trade transactions. Before upgrading this software, a total of more than 1 billion dollars traded with 0 defaults. This software had the functionality to link with all the clearing banks so that balance, pay-in and payout instruction will be requested from the settlement banks automatically in a secure way. The clearing module of this software calculates the total net obligation per each member and client that traded for the specified trade date. It also calculates fees and charges that the exchange deducts from each trade. The software has another section which is responsible for calculating the net obligation and settlement instruction for inventory financed commodities
Designed, developed and implemented ECX-trading application software which the trading department uses as a tool for processing trade data. Submitted trades will be verified with more than 25 trade status checks. It has a module which is optimized for fast and automatic verification of each trade which based on this verification a trade becomes accepted or rejected. The software is capable of identifying trades that does not meet the exchange's requirements.
Designed and developed ECX-warehouse application. The software was used in ECX warehouses in order to sample, grade, load and create Good Receiving Notice (GRN).It has also another section for tracking Good Issue Notice (GIN) information.
Maintained and enhanced the above developed software application as required by the exchange.
Tools and language used- C#, VB6.0, SQL Server 2005, MS Access

This software has been used in determining the future number of schools to be constructed taking into account School size, a radius that a student travels to the nearest school and school-aged population in the region.
There are others different educational software developed during the period Jan 01-Dec 03. Such as Decision Support system that shows the previous trend analysis on key educational indicators

publications: Enhanced Human-Robot Interaction through Spatio-Temporal Saliency Prediction: A Stacked-ConvLSTM Approach with XY-Shift Frame Differencing. Springer Nature of Computer Science. 2023. Spatio-temporal saliency prediction, with its broad applications ranging from human-robot interaction to social standard robotics, plays a significant role in computer vision. Traditional static saliency prediction techniques are inadequate for video saliency prediction, necessitating the utilization of deep learning approaches that leverage spatio-temporal saliency. However, deep learning techniques encounter challenges due to the complex nature of video saliency prediction and the scarcity of representative saliency benchmarks and architectures designed specifically for video saliency. In this paper, we propose a novel video saliency prediction model that incorporates a stacked-ConvLSTM-based architecture along with a custom XY-shift frame differencing layer. Our model adopts an encoder-decoder structure, incorporating a prior layer for XY-shift frame differencing, a residual layer that combines spatially processed features based on the VGG-16 model with XY-shift frame-differenced frames, and a stacked-ConvLSTM component. Furthermore, we apply our video saliency prediction model to enhance human-robot interaction. We conducted comprehensive evaluations and comparisons with state-of-the-art static and dynamic video saliency prediction models, demonstrating competitive results. Additionally, subjective ratings and latency metrics were employed to evaluate the performance of our model in human-robot interaction scenarios using the Pepper Robot, both in virtual and physical environments. The obtained promising results support the efficacy of enhancing human-robot interaction through saliency prediction models. Finally, we suggest potential research areas that may be of interest to researchers in this field. Our work contributes to the advancement of video saliency prediction and highlights its importance in enhancing human-robot interaction.

Anthropomorphic Human Robot Interaction: Attention Based Approach. RoboCup Symposium, IEEE - Accepted. 2023. For human-robot interaction (HRI) to be both intuitive and efficient, robots must be capable of identifying crucial environmental cues in the same manner as conscious beings. Human attention models enable agents to understand these important environmental cues by simulating the way humans selectively attend to and process visual information. This selective nature of human attention models makes them useful as heuristic functions for heavier computer vision models by identifying regions of interest in an image or video and letting heavier vision models to focus only on those regions, thereby reducing the computational resources required. This paper presents the role of attention models in creating anthropomorphic HRI. We use saliency model and a moving object detection model together as a human attention model in order to improve the intuitiveness and efficiency of HRI. Our framework is built using a robot operating system (ROS) framework and on top of the humanoid robot, Pepper. Due to the qualitative and quantitative nature of the problem, we undertook Subjective ratings, Task completion time, and Error rate measure to evaluate the HRI. Furthermore, a quantitative measure both models in our human attention sub system are evaluated against their corresponding state-of-art research works. Our extensive experiments show the significance of our framework both in enabling intuitive human-robot interaction and reducing computational overhead of other resource-intensive computer vision tasks.

Interactive Video Saliency Prediction: The Stacked-convLSTM Approach. Wondimu, N., Visser, U. and Buche, C. In Proceedings of the 15th ICAART. 2023. Cognitive and neuroscience of attention researches suggest the use of spatio-temporal features for an efficient video saliency prediction. This is due to the representative nature of spatio-temporal features for data collected across space and time, such as videos. Video saliency prediction aims to find visually salient regions in a stream of images. Many video saliency prediction models are proposed in the past couple of years. Due to the unique nature of videos from that of static images, the earliest efforts to employ static image saliency prediction models for video saliency prediction task yield reduced performance. Consequently, dynamic video saliency prediction models that use spatio-temporal features were introduced. These models, especially deep learning based video saliency prediction models, transformed the state-of-the-art of video saliency prediction to a better level. However, video saliency prediction still remains a considerable challenge. This has been mainly due to the complex nature of video saliency prediction and scarcity of representative saliency benchmarks. Given the importance of saliency identification for various computer vision tasks, revising and enhancing the performance of video saliency prediction models is crucial. To this end, we propose a novel interactive video saliency prediction model that employs stacked-ConvLSTM based architecture along with a novel XY-shift frame differencing custom layer. Specifically, we introduce an encoder-decoder based architecture with a prior layer undertaking XY-shift frame differencing, a residual layer fusing spatially processed (VGG-16 based) features with XY-shift frame differenced frames, and a stacked-ConvLSTM component. Extensive experimental results over the largest video saliency dataset, DHF1K, show the competitive performance of our model against the state-of-the-art models.

A New Approach to Moving Object Detection and Segmentation: The XY-shift Frame Differencing. Wondimu, N., Visser, U. and Buche, C. In Proceedings of the 15th ICAART 2023. Motion out-weights other low-level saliency features in attracting human attention and defining region of interests. The ability to effectively identify moving objects in a sequence of frames help to solve important computer vision problems, such as moving object detection and segmentation. In this paper, we propose a novel frame differencing technique along with a simple three-stream encoder-decoder architecture to effectively and efficiently detect and segment moving objects in a sequence of frames. Our frame differencing component incorporates a novel self-differencing technique, which we call XY-shift frame differencing, and an improved three-frame differencing technique. We fuse the feature maps from the raw frame and the two outputs of our frame differencing component, and fed them to our transfer-learning based convolutional base, VGG-16. The result from this sub-component is further deconvolved and the desired segmentation map is produced. The effectiveness of our model is evaluated using the re-labeled multi-spectral CDNet-2014 dataset for motion segmentation. The qualitative and quantitative results show that our technique achieves effective and efficient moving object detection and segmentation results relative to the state-of-the-art methods.

Interactive machine learning: A state of the art review. Wondimu, N. A., Buche, C., and Visser, U. ArXiv preprint arXiv:2207.06196 (2022). Machine learning has proved useful in many software disciplines, including computer vision, speech and audio processing, natural language processing, robotics and some other fields. However, its applicability has been significantly hampered due its black-box nature and significant resource consumption. Performance is achieved at the expense of enormous computational resource and usually compromising the robustness and trustworthiness of the model. Recent researches have been identifying a lack of interactivity as the prime source of these machine learning problems. Consequently, interactive machine learning (iML) has acquired increased attention of researchers on account of its human-in-the-loop modality and relatively efficient resource utilization. Thereby, a state-of-the-art review of interactive machine learning plays a vital role in easing the effort toward building human-centred models. In this paper, we provide a comprehensive analysis of the state-of-the-art of iML. We analyze salient research works using merit-oriented and application/task oriented mixed taxonomy. We use a bottom-up clustering approach to generate a taxonomy of iML research works. Research works on adversarial black-box attacks and corresponding iML based defense system, exploratory machine learning, resource constrained learning, and iML performance evaluation are analyzed under their corresponding theme in our merit-oriented taxonomy. We have further classified these research works into technical and sectoral categories. Finally, research opportunities that we believe are inspiring for future work in iML are discussed thoroughly.

employment.description_of_duties:
The office represents the free state of Bavaria across four cooperation areas which are development, economic, scientific, and cultural within the five focus African countries (Ethiopia, Senegal, Kenya, Tunisia, and South Africa). My responsibilities include;
• Conceive, initiate, and develop cooperation projects in areas such as TVET support, forestry, business development, agriculture and manufacturing industry development, health, and more.

• Consulting Bavarian SMEs interested in expanding to Africa, providing insight into regional markets.
• Creating and sending frequent reports on the development of strategic sectors such as green hydrogen and other renewables, automotive (e-mobility in particular), and finance to the Bavarian Ministry of Economic Affairs.
• Creating links between Ethiopian/ African and Bavarian Institutions (such as universities, research institutions, government offices, and CSOs) and facilitating their collaboration.
• Representing and promoting Ethiopia and Africa in economic forums and events in Bavaria such as the Bavaria-Africa forum, IFAT, and others.
• Monitoring, researching, and reporting on political and socio-economic developments across the focus countries and the African Union.
• Organization of cultural events and delegation trips to Africa by the Bavarian State Government or the Bavarian Parliament

As part of the Sustainable Industrial Clusters (S.I.C.) program, I was mainly tasked with developing a new manual for Industrial Parks One Stop Shop (OSS) service centers and conducting market research on available operational OSS platforms. Some of our work include, proposals for a regional poultry sector development project by the Amhara regional government to develop business models and an investment case for selected intervention areas in the poultry sector.
• Drafting inception reports, designing surveys, overseeing data collection, conducting interviews and focus group discussions, coding and analysis of qualitative data. I have been a part of research spanning various subjects such as impact of covid on the Ethiopian MICE industry, e-governance and doing business, and constraints blocking domestic investors from entering IPs.
• Conduct feasibility studies and create investment decks.

• Consulting investors on the possible investment avenues in Ethiopia (pros and cons), and the procedures involved in licensing and registration and different services provided by relevant institutions.
• Developing business plans for enterprises in pre-implementation and expansion phases. I have worked on business plans for companies in the textile, construction material manufacturing, industrial parks development, leather and real-estate sectors.
• I was the content manager for IPortal, an online platform designed to provide detailed information on the investment process in Ethiopia (especially for FDI). I have drafted a content strategy and taken part in writing and editing over 30 articles for the website. As IPortal is a platform designed to provide information on the investment process in Ethiopia, the articles are focused on investor FAQs and the steps investors (and especially FDI) will have to go through to become operational in Ethiopia (beginning from arriving in Ethiopia).
A Sample of articles I have written: https://investin.et/industrial-park-developer/ , https://investin.et/the-investment-climate-in-ethiopia/
• As the content manager I have hired and manage a team of writers and legal analysts. I have designed a training program (including introduction to investment in Ethiopia, copywriting and search engine optimization), actively monitor their progress, and manage their tasks using digital tools such as click up and slack.

• Develop a baseline assessment tools for the Tristate IGA project: the project is designed to provide training and funding to women from low-income families.
• Conduct a market opportunity assessment in areas the women live in and support them in setting up a business of their choice.
• Assist in the financial literacy and soft-skills training provided for the women.
• Follow up and conduct a bimonthly assessment to report on their progress and challenges they are facing.

• Reviewing the feasibility studies of various potential industrial park entrants guided them through developing a feasibility study when needed. When found to have fulfilled all entry requirements, presenting the projects to the deputy commissioner of the Industrial parks division for approval
• Facilitating the incorporation of entrants into Industrial Parks. This involves investor hand holding through-out the pre-implementation, implementation and operational stages. Some common tasks include providing information such as investment and industrial park laws, incentives available to them and procedural requirements at different government; and accompanying them to different institutions (such as NBE, IPDC, and EFDA) representing EIC. I have worked with many of the new companies entering Kilinto Industrial Park, Adama Industrial Park and the Integrated Agro-Industrial Parks.
• Served as a park lead for Hawassa and Vogue Industrial Parks. Furthermore, I was appointed key account manager for over 36 companies. Through this role I have represented and supported Industrial Parks tenants in negotiating with different government institutions to solve their issues. INVEA, Customs Commission, Ethiopian Food and Drug Authority and Industrial Parks Development Corporation are some of the institutions I regularly dealt with.
• Prepared and provided presentations on different industrial parks to foreign investors and taken them on tour of various IPs.
• Consulting duties in the drafting of the Industrial Parks land allocation directive.
• Initiated and co-led the Industrial Parks Division Profiling project. This project was aimed at mapping the history of the IP Division within EIC, its key achievements and shortcomings, challenges, mandates of the directorates within the division (as there was some clashing of mandates at the time), collaborations with other divisions and institutions and recommendations to fill the gaps.

employment.description_of_duties:
Project #1: FinHub – Digital Banking Solution (Jan 2023 - Present)
Role: Senior Developer (Fintech)
FinHub is a tenant-based digital banking platform designed to serve multiple financial institutions as independent tenants. SEPA Cyber is the main service provider, offering the core banking infrastructure and payment solutions, while each registered institution can subscribe to a variety of banking products tailored to their needs. This platform allows seamless integration with banking services and provides a secure, scalable, and flexible solution for institutions to deliver digital banking services to their customers.
Responsibilities:
• Developed backend components from the ground up to support a multi-tenant architecture, allowing each financial institution (tenant) to operate independently within the same platform.
• Work with a microservices-based architecture to modularize backend components, ensuring scalability and maintainability.
• Developed and implemented robust APIs for facilitating secure money transfers between customers, ensuring compliance with industry standards and regulatory requirements.
• Designed and optimized backend systems to support efficient processing of withdrawal and payment transactions via card, enhancing overall system performance and scalability
• Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests
Environment: Golang, Java, Spring boot, Kafka, Redis, gRPC, MariaDB (MYSQL), Ballerina, Kuberentes, Docker, Confluence and Scrum methodology.

Project #2: Smart Coin Management (Aug 2022 – Jan 2023)
Role: Senior Developer
Smart Coin Management is a tenant-based digital asset management platform designed to support business customers with fiat-to-crypto conversion and trading functionalities. The main service provider oversees the infrastructure, offering integrated payment solutions and regulatory compliance, while each business customer, or tenant, manages their interactions within the system. Through this platform, business customers can deposit fiat currency via an Integrated Payment Gateway (IPG), and upon approval, they receive a local ERC20 token representing their deposit. After the tenant verifies and approves the transaction using smart contracts deployed on a blockchain network, the customer can swap the local token for USDT (a popular stable coin), enabling them to engage in trading activities on the integrated trading platform.
Responsibilities:
• Built a client application interface for business customers to manage deposits, swaps, and approvals.
• Integrated blockchain smart contracts with the backend to automate the token issuance and swapping processes.
• Deployed and managed smart contracts on the blockchain, ensuring reliable and secure transaction processing.
• Designed and implemented approval workflows for tenant-initiated transactions, enabling secure swaps and compliance with platform requirements.
Environment: Typescript, Node.js, Next.js, Web3.js, Polygon (Blockchain), Alchemy (Node infra provider) Kafka, Redis, MariaDB, Docker, Remix, Kuberentes, and Confluence.

Project #4: Integrated Commodity Exchange Regulatory System (ICERS) (Nov 2019 - Aug 2022)
Role: Full Stack Developer
The Integrated Commodity Exchange Regulatory System (ICERS) is a platform designed to promote transparency, fairness, and efficiency in electronic trading activities within the commodity exchange market. It enhances the oversight capacity of exchange actors, allowing for more effective monitoring and regulation to prevent market manipulation and ensure compliance with industry standards. ICERS also supports data-driven decision-making, empowering regulatory bodies with the insights needed to make timely and informed decisions. By strengthening oversight mechanisms and providing reliable trading data, ICERS builds confidence among both clients and exchange participants, contributing to a more robust and trustworthy commodity exchange ecosystem. This project includes the following modules:
• Licensing Module
• Law Enforcement Module
• Surveillance Module
Responsibilities:
• Involved in the design, coding, and Documentation of the project from the ground up.
• Developed and maintained front-end components of the Licensing and Surveillance Module, including user interfaces, data visualization tools, and interactive dashboards.
• Implemented back-end services and APIs to support core platform functionality, including data processing(surveillance), Licensing, user authentication, and authorization.
• Interacting with the Business/Product Owner through planning, grooming meetings, and understanding the requirements.
Environment: Dot.Net, Angular, MSSQL, and DevExpress

Project #3: Human Resources Information Management (HRIS-Licensing) (May 2022 - Sept 2022)
Role: Full Stack Developer
It is a platform developed for the Ethiopian Ministry of Health to provide a centralized repository of employee master data that the human resource management group needs for completing core human resource processes.
Responsibilities:
• Designed and developed the front-end interface of the Human resource management system (HRMS), including user dashboards, employee profiles, recruitment portals, and administrative tools.
• Integrated with back-end APIs and services to enable seamless data exchange between the front-end and the underlying database, ensuring data consistency, security, and privacy.
Environment: React.js, Node.js, Vue.js, PostgreSQL, and Kibana.

Project #5: Smart Electric Meter Management System (SMMS) (Sep 2018 - Nov 2019)
Role: Software Engineer
The Smart Electric Meter Management System (SMMS) is an advanced data management solution designed to automate and optimize the monitoring of power grids and Smart electric meters. It transitions utilities from traditional, manual metering systems to state-of-the-art, smart metering technologies that provide real-time monitoring and management of consumption data. By integrating smart meters, SIMS enables utilities to collect detailed usage data, detect issues such as energy theft or inefficiencies, and improve overall grid.
Responsibilities:
• Implemented RESTful APIs and backend services to facilitate communication between the metering devices, data storage systems, and user interfaces, ensuring seamless integration, and data flow across the system.
• Developed automated tests and continuous integration pipelines to validate the functionality, performance, and reliability of backend services, ensuring high software quality and stability.
• Fixing bugs/issues reported by the production team.
Environment: Java, Python, Spring, MYSQL, Jenkins, and JMeter

_extracted.professional_summary: Mussie Tsegaye is an Ethiopian development professional with multidisciplinary expertise in Ease of Doing Business, investment facilitation, private sector development, digitalization, project management, and digital entrepreneurship. He brings extensive experience in the design, implementation and monitoring of development cooperation programmes specific to economic development, industrialization and digitalization in Ethiopia, having worked with numerous government institutions at federal and local levels and multiple international donors including the EU, World Bank, GIZ, FCDO, and Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation. With a strong understanding of the political, economic and organizational dynamics of development projects in the region and a wide network within the public and private sector ecosystem in Ethiopia, he has hands-on experience in research and implementation of projects across multiple sectors including EODB, agriculture, agro-processing, textile and apparel manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, and ICT. Currently serving as an Ease of Doing Business Specialist with the World Bank and as founder of Envest Technologies PLC, a consulting firm specializing in ICT, digitalization, business, investment, and economic development.

employment.position: STC - Ease of Doing Business Specialist
employment.description_of_duties:
I support the National Ease of Doing Business Technical and Steering Committees in crafting the second Ease of Doing Business Agenda. Specifically:
- I provided technical support to the Technical Committee in assessing the status of previous Ease of Doing Reforms in terms of current status, impact and way forward
- I supported the Technical Committee convert issues raised through different Public Private Dialogue Platforms into actionable reform items that will be part of the second Ease of Doing Business Reform Plan of the Government of Ethiopia
- I supported the coordination of EoDB implementing institutions on a continuous basis in monitoring and reporting to the technical and steering committees
- I supported technical committee in compiling and presenting weekly reform updates and following up on the decisions of the steering committee

employment.employer: EU Funded GIZ InS Project, Business regulatory reforms and investment climate
employment.description_of_duties:
The EU funded BEIC project aims to improve the Ease of Doing Business and Investment Climate of Ethiopia. My role as an EODB Expert included the assessment, improvement and development of different EODB initiatives of the government including:
− Supporting the Assessment, proposal and enhancement/development of different EODB initiatives of the government including digital service provision E-Portals
− Support the 'Starting a Business' EODB Indicator in terms of diagnosis and support on Online Trade Registration and Licensing System of the Ministry of Trade and Regional Integration, propose upgrades to the system in order to improve the ranking in terms of reducing the number of days to start a business. Specifically, our team proposed and implemented an integration with different stakeholders in the business registration process including MoTRI, DARA, National ID, CCIB Issuing bodies and MoR.
− Support the 'Paying Taxes' EODB Indicator in terms of diagnosis and support on e-Filing and e-Payment solutions for tax administration system at the Ministry of Revenues. Specifically, my team supported the MoR in improving the indicator through the upgrade of the E-Filing system significantly reducing the file upload speed and digitalizing the Clearance and Refund systems among other support areas.
− Support the 'Access to Credit' EODB Indicator on the enhancement/improvement of Credit Registry Bureau and Movable Collateral Asset Registry systems at the Central Bank of Ethiopia. Specifically, our team diagnosed the existing Credit Bureau and Movable Collateral Asset registry systems and supported the NBE in improving its credit scoring activities.
− Support on Training Needs Analysis, capacity building and strategy development for improvement of service provision pertaining to EoDB indicators and cross cutting digital e-services activities at different ministries including the MINT, MOR, NBE and MoTRI
− Provide capacity building on different EODB services provision and digital capacity building activities for all ministries including technical, project management, security, cybersecurity trainings (including training of trainers) and study tours to two destinations

employment.employer: Envest Technologies PLC (formerly Envest ICT Consultancy)

We oversee and manage business and technology projects for multiple clients. Our company provides the following broad category of services
− Trade and Investment Advisory and Support

− Design and Implementation of Development Projects

We are a member of Alibaba's eFounders Fellowship Program for Africa (2020/21) and China Merchant Group's C-STAR Programme (2023). As of June, 2024, we are a designated Africa branch and secretary general office for Ethiopia and Djibouti of the Shenzhen Cross-Border E-Commerce Association which is the largest Cross-Border E-Commerce association of more than 3000 Chinese E-Commerce Companies and we work with E-Commerce Companies to improve the E-Commerce Ecosystem in Ethiopia and Beyond.

employment.position: Private Sector Development Specialist, Competitive Neutrality Assessment on the Agricultural Sector in Ethiopia (Fertilizer and Seed Sectors)
employment.description_of_duties:
I supported the private sector analysis (including data collection) of research on the fertilizer and hybrid seed value chains in Ethiopia, the study included the following aspects of both State-Owned Enterprises (SOEs) and private actors in the two sectors:
− Background on the sector: market size, trends, structure, main players, sector prospects, challenges and regulatory environment of the seed and fertilizer sectors
− Value chain assessment and analysis of market characteristics (including production, distribution and sales, prices, principal characteristics of downstream product markets, with particular focus on the role of SOEs vis a vis private sector players along the value chain).
− Assessment of government interventions (regulations/policies) that may restrict competition along the seed value chain
− Policy recommendation to improve the competitiveness of the two markets based on the inefficiencies identified through the assessments

employment.position: Industry Park Services Advisor and Digitalization Expert (Team Leader)
employment.description_of_duties:
I lead a team of three experts including a senior systems analyst and an investment specialist and managed to:
Developed an Industrial Park Consolidated One Stop Shop Manual including:
- Mapping the detailed process flows including steps and required documents for more than 160 services being provided by more than 15 service providers for investors in Industrial Parks
- Organizing the process maps into a consolidated OSS Manual document which is being used as a reference for any investor who plans to or has already invested in Ethiopia.
Undertook a Market Research, Business Process Analysis and system requirement specification preparation for OSS Digital System including:
- Analysis of existing systems which are currently providing online services to investors by more than 15 service providers across more than 10 special economic zones including the services provided, specific functions, gaps, integration between systems a as well as proposed improvements for a seamless service provision to investors.
- Detail assessment and gap analysis of new and existing services as well as business processes that should be re-engineered in order to be integrated into or interfaced with an envisaged OSS digital system

employment.position: Lead Consultant – Study on the challenges and opportunities for domestic firms in Industrial Parks (Textile, Agro-processing and Pharmaceutical sectors)
employment.description_of_duties:
− Lead a study to identify challenges and opportunities in Special Economic Zones for domestic investors in Ethiopia.
− Undertake a survey and qualitative assessment of more than 25 firms in the Textile and Apparel, Agro-processing and pharmaceutical sectors within and out of Industrial parks to identify opportunities and the most pressing challenges in the process of investing in Industrial Parks in Ethiopia
− Undertake a survey and analysis of multiple public institutions including the Investment Commission, Ministry of Industry, Industrial Parks Development Corporation and others to flesh out the critical bottlenecks that investors face and validate potential recommendations to boost domestic investment in Industrial Parks
− Propose policy, regulatory and operational recommendations based on the findings and pitch to the relevant government bodies, primarily the Ministry of Industry

− Liaise with the EIC and Ministry of Foreign Affairs to float Bole Lemi II and Kilinto as a proposal ready for leasing to overseas Ethiopian Embassies and to foreign missions in Addis.
− Provide support to an international WB outreach Textile and Apparel and Pharmaceuticals teams in terms of gathering and compiling critical information required to pitch and attract investors from different government institutions
− Due diligence on potential international investors and follow up on investor queries in order to create a pipeline and facilitate information exchange with the relevant authorities with the ultimate aim of supporting investors to set up in Kilinto and Bole Lemi II Industry Parks

employment.employer: EU Funded GIZ InS Project, Business regulatory reforms and investment climate
employment.description_of_duties:

− Support finalization of the project's initial work-plan with beneficiaries and ensure that there is no duplication of work between the various initiatives of the EODB programme
− Support consultation with implementing ministries/agencies to identify further EODB support options and develop and implement support activities, focusing on digitalisation and modernization of government E-Services.
− Support the preparation of the inception report for the project based on the inputs gathered through engagements with the aforementioned stakeholders.

employment.position: Donor Engagement, Business Development, and Market Assessment Consultant
employment.description_of_duties:
− Assess of the potential client landscape and market potential for GIZ InS including to European Union, World Bank, African Development Bank, African Union, Foundations, and bilateral Donors.
− Analysis of the programming priorities and identification of relevant funding modalities of potential clients/donors.
− Provide recommendations for business expansion and concrete plan for engagement including a long/short list of possible projects to bid for and how to engage clients productively.

− Lead the periodic program monitoring sessions of the program coordination unit.
− Lead the monitoring and reporting of investment specific results of the program.
− Play a major role on the preparation of the program operations manual and communications manual for the program.

− Manage/lead the establishment of a GIZ funded centre of excellence at Hawassa Industrial Park. I led the execution of the project including operations, finance and procurement, project monitoring and reporting. The centre was established to provide different trainings to industry park tenants and factory personnel on quality assurance and supervision with the ultimate aim of improving production and productivity of firms within the park.
− Engaged in the operationalization of the Jobs Compact program which is a World Bank, DFID, and EU funded program aiming to hire 30,000 refugees and 70,000 Ethiopians within and beyond Industrial parks. I was involved in the establishment of the program monitoring unit (including recruitment and procurement).
− Engaged in the implementation of a Bill and Melinda Gates foundation funded project with aims of strengthening investor engagement and regulatory powers of the commission. I was responsible for implementation, periodic fund utilization and activity report pertaining to the project.
− Engaged in the implementation of a EU funded project, PE I and PE II of the TTF (Transformation Triggering Facilities) project, which aimed to build the infrastructural as well as human capacity of the Investment Commission. I led and was also involved in ToR preparation and tender dossier preparation, evaluation and procurement of different activities under the project.
− Follow up on specific investment projects as a key account manager. This includes supporting and facilitating for the companies at all phases including pre-licensing, licensing and implementation.
− Play a role in a restructuring of the commission. I was part of a team that diagnosed the institutional arrangement of the commission and proposed an adjustment to the existing structure of the commission.
− Play a major role in the design and implementation of a DFID (UK Aid) funded Young Professional's Program. I was part of the team that designed the program, recruited 15 young professionals and implemented the program.

− Manage and upgrade the structure and content of the Commission's Website. I worked closely with the ICT and PR directorates to upgrade the website and integrate different functionalities including analytics, client feedback, and keyword optimization.
− Design and prepare a bi-monthly investment newsletter for internal staff consumption.
− Support in the Identification and screening of prospective international textile and apparel manufacturers in line with the Ethiopian Investment strategy.
− Play a supporting role in the initiation and establishment of a new strategic management unit at the commission. I was closely involved in the planning, proposal, staffing and operationalization of the unit. This included the diagnosis of the Planning and Reporting system within the commission.

− Develop and deployed content management systems and websites for the company
− Work in a group that specialized in data mining and analysis using machine learning algorithms, specifically I worked on chat bots responsible for medical diagnosis and keeping company of old age patients.

− Installing and configuring network infrastructure
− Developing and Deploying content management systems and websites
− Providing training and support to end-users
− Presenting sales pitch to prospective clients

education.degree: MSc in Electrical and Computer Engineering
BSc in Electrical and Computer Engineering

employment.description_of_duties:
Implemented AI tools for the inspection of exterior damage on cars, reducing the time required for preparing condition reports from 12 minutes to 3 minutes. This work streamlined the inspection process, boosting productivity and enhancing accuracy in damage assessment.
Conducted in-depth research on improving 3D reconstruction, pose estimation and novel view synthesis techniques from sparse observations.

Developed a multi-stage machine learning model to replace the traditional vision pipeline that had been in place for over five years, enhancing system reliability and performance.
Architected a scenario-based evaluation framework to enhance the explainability and interpretability of in-house machine learning models. This framework improved model transparency and aided in the validation and adjustment of model outputs, making them more understandable and actionable for end-users.
Boosted on-edge LiDAR processing capabilities by optimizing firmware and streamlining data throughput, significantly improving 3D data handling efficiency.
Created tools for efficient hyperparameter tuning that reduced development cycles and enhanced the machine learning models' responsiveness to dynamic environments.

Developed ultra-low latency pedestrian detection systems for automatic emergency braking (AEB), utilizing event cameras and LiDAR technology to enhance rapid response capabilities.
Introduced a method to fuse images with radar data, offering a low-cost alternative to traditional LiDAR systems without sacrificing performance.
Generated synthetic datasets through simulations of diverse sensors in the CARLA environment, for use in pretraining large transformer models.

Developed deep learning based detection algorithms for company's novel and cutting-edge solid state LiDARs.
Explored and pioneered transformer-based models into existing 3D object detection pipeline, setting a new standard for object recognition accuracy.

Engineered a streamlined real-time 3D object detection system that reduced computational load by more than 80% while maintaining robust performance on the edge.
Implemented a critical project on model compression, pruning, quantization, low-rank factorization and distillation, achieving a significant reduction in model size with minimal loss in accuracy, enhancing the deployment capabilities on resource-constrained platforms.

Developed drivers and libraries for interfacing with humanoid robots
Used genetic algorithms for generating realistic facial expressions
Devised systematic methodology for collecting and annotating sequences of emotional speech, facial expressions and body gesture
